subject: Biology
title: The Light Reactions
description: A short lesson to help you apply The Light Reactions.

hook.text: Inside every leaf, chloroplasts capture photons of sunlight and convert them into chemical energy.

buildup.text: Chlorophyll absorbs red and blue light but reflects green—that's why leaves look green.

discovery.text: Light energy splits water molecules, releasing oxygen as a byproduct.

twist.text: The oxygen you breathe is a waste product of photosynthesis. Plants discard it; you need it to live.

climax.text: The light reactions produce ATP and NADPH—energy carriers used in the next stage.

punchline.text: Plants throw away what keeps you alive.

quiz.question: Why do leaves appear green?
options: Green pigment is a waste product
